What is reinforcement learning?

Reinforcement learning (RL) is a type of machine learning where an agent learns to make decisions by interacting with an environment to maximise cumulative reward.

Core concepts

  • Agent — the learner or decision-maker
  • Environment — what the agent interacts with
  • Reward — the feedback signal guiding learning
import gymnasium as gym

env = gym.make('CartPole-v1')
obs, _ = env.reset()
for _ in range(1000):
    action = env.action_space.sample()
    obs, reward, done, _, _ = env.step(action)
    if done:
        obs, _ = env.reset()
How does it differ from supervised learning?

In supervised learning the model trains on labelled examples where the correct answer is provided upfront. In reinforcement learning there are no labels — the agent discovers good behaviour through trial and error guided by reward signals.
